diacritized_text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ أُذَيْنَةَ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ عُتْبَةَ الْهَاشِمِيِّ قَالَ كُنْتُ قَاعِداً عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ بِمَكَّةَ إِذْ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ أُنَاسٌ مِنَ الْمُعْتَزِلَةِ فِيهِمْ عَمْرُو بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ وَ وَاصِلُ بْنُ عَطَاءٍ وَ حَفْصُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ مَوْلَى ابْنِ هُبَيْرَةَ وَ نَاسٌ مِنْ رُؤَسَائِهِمْ وَ ذَلِكَ حِدْثَانُ قَتْلِ الْوَلِيدِ وَ اخْتِلَافِ أَهْلِ الشَّامِ بَيْنَهُمْ فَتَكَلَّمُوا وَ أَكْثَرُوا وَ خَطَبُوا فَأَطَالُوا فَقَالَ لَهُمْ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِنَّكُمْ قَدْ أَكْثَرْتُمْ عَلَيَّ فَأَسْنِدُوا أَمْرَكُمْ إِلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْكُمْ وَ لْيَتَكَلَّمْ بِحُجَجِكُمْ وَ يُوجِزُ فَأَسْنَدُوا أَمْرَهُمْ إِلَى عَمْرِو بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ فَتَكَلَّمَ فَأَبْلَغَ وَ أَطَالَ فَكَانَ فِيمَا قَالَ أَنْ قَالَ قَدْ قَتَلَ أَهْلُ الشَّامِ خَلِيفَتَهُمْ وَ ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ وَ شَتَّتَ اللَّهُ أَمْرَهُمْ فَنَظَرْنَا فَوَجَدْنَا رَجُلًا لَهُ دِينٌ وَ عَقْلٌ وَ مُرُوَّةٌ وَ مَوْضِعٌ وَ مَعْدِنٌ لِلْخِلَافَةِ وَ هُوَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ فَأَرَدْنَا أَنْ نَجْتَمِعَ عَلَيْهِ فَنُبَايِعَهُ ثُمَّ نَظْهَرَ مَعَهُ فَمَنْ كَانَ بَايَعَنَا فَهُوَ مِنَّا وَ كُنَّا مِنْهُ وَ مَنِ اعْتَزَلَنَا كَفَفْنَا عَنْهُ وَ مَنْ نَصَبَ لَنَا جَاهَدْنَاهُ وَ نَصَبْنَا لَهُ عَلَى بَغْيِهِ وَ رَدِّهِ إِلَى الْحَقِّ وَ أَهْلِهِ وَ قَدْ أَحْبَبْنَا أَنْ نَعْرِضَ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْكَ فَتَدْخُلَ مَعَنَا فَإِنَّهُ لَا غِنًى بِنَا عَنْ مِثْلِكَ لِمَوْضِعِكَ وَ كَثْرَةِ شِيعَتِكَ فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ أَ كُلُّكُمْ عَلَى مِثْلِ مَا قَالَ عَمْرٌو قَالُوا نَعَمْ فَحَمِدَ اللَّهَ وَ أَثْنَى عَلَيْهِ وَ صَلَّى عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّمَا نَسْخَطُ إِذَا عُصِيَ اللَّهُ فَأَمَّا إِذَا أُطِيعَ رَضِينَا أَخْبِرْنِي يَا عَمْرُو لَوْ أَنَّ الْأُمَّةَ قَلَّدَتْكَ أَمْرَهَا وَ وَلَّتْكَ بِغَيْرِ قِتَالٍ وَ لَا مَئُونَةٍ وَ قِيلَ لَكَ وَ لِّهَا مَنْ شِئْتَ مَنْ كُنْتَ تُوَلِّيهَا قَالَ كُنْتُ أَجْعَلُهَا شُورَى بَيْنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ قَالَ بَيْنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ كُلِّهِمْ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ بَيْنَ فُقَهَائِهِمْ وَ خِيَارِهِمْ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ قُرَيْشٍ وَ غَيْرِهِمْ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ وَ الْعَرَبِ وَ الْعَجَمِ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ أَخْبِرْنِي يَا عَمْرُو أَ تَتَوَلَّى أَبَا بَكْرٍ وَ عُمَرَ أَوْ تَتَبَرَّأُ مِنْهُمَا قَالَ أَتَوَلَّاهُمَا فَقَالَ فَقَدْ خَالَفْتَهُمَا مَا تَقُولُونَ أَنْتُمْ تَتَوَلَّوْنَهُمَا أَوْ تَتَبَرَّءُونَ مِنْهُمَا قَالُوا نَتَوَلَّاهُمَا قَالَ يَا عَمْرُو إِنْ كُنْتَ رَجُلًا تَتَبَرَّأُ مِنْهُمَا فَإِنَّهُ يَجُوزُ لَكَ الْخِلَافُ عَلَيْهِمَا وَ إِنْ كُنْتَ تَتَوَلَّاهُمَا فَقَدْ خَالَفْتَهُمَا قَدْ عَهِدَ عُمَرُ إِلَى أَبِي بَكْرٍ فَبَايَعَهُ وَ لَمْ يُشَاوِرْ فِيهِ أَحَداً ثُمَّ رَدَّهَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ عَلَيْهِ وَ لَمْ يُشَاوِرْ فِيهِ أَحَداً ثُمَّ جَعَلَهَا عُمَرُ شُورَى بَيْنَ سِتَّةٍ وَ أَخْرَجَ مِنْهَا جَمِيعَ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَ الْأَنْصَارِ غَيْرَ أُولَئِكَ السِّتَّةِ مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ وَ أَوْصَى فِيهِمْ شَيْئاً لَا أَرَاكَ تَرْضَى بِهِ أَنْتَ وَ لَا أَصْحَابُكَ إِذْ جَعَلْتَهَا شُورَى بَيْنَ جَمِيعِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ قَالَ وَ مَا صَنَعَ قَالَ أَمَرَ صُهَيْباً أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بِالنَّاسِ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ وَ أَنْ يُشَاوِرَ أُولَئِكَ السِّتَّةَ لَيْسَ مَعَهُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا ابْنُ عُمَرَ يُشَاوِرُونَهُ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ وَ أَوْصَى مَنْ بِحَضْرَتِهِ مِنَ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ وَ الْأَنْصَارِ إِنْ مَضَتْ ثَلَاثَةُ أَيَّامٍ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَفْرُغُوا أَوْ يُبَايِعُوا رَجُلًا أَنْ يَضْرِبُوا أَعْنَاقَ أُولَئِكَ السِّتَّةِ جَمِيعاً فَإِنِ اجْتَمَعَ أَرْبَعَةٌ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَمْضِيَ ثَلَاثَةُ أَيَّامٍ وَ خَالَفَ اثْنَانِ أَنْ يَضْرِبُوا أَعْنَاقَ الِاثْنَيْنِ أَ فَتَرْضَوْنَ بِهَذَا أَنْتُمْ فِيمَا تَجْعَلُونَ مِنَ الشُّورَى فِي جَمَاعَةٍ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ قَالُوا لَا ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا عَمْرُو دَعْ ذَا أَ رَأَيْتَ لَوْ بَايَعْتُ صَاحِبَكَ الَّذِي تَدْعُونِي إِلَى بَيْعَتِهِ ثُمَّ اجْتَمَعَتْ لَكُمُ الْأُمَّةُ فَلَمْ يَخْتَلِفْ عَلَيْكُمْ رَجُلَانِ فِيهَا فَأَفَضْتُمْ إِلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُسْلِمُونَ وَ لَا يُؤَدُّونَ الْجِزْيَةَ أَ كَانَ عِنْدَكُمْ وَ عِنْدَ صَاحِبِكُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ مَا تَسِيرُونَ بِسِيرَةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي الْمُشْرِكِينَ فِي حُرُوبِهِ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَتَصْنَعُ مَا ذَا قَالَ نَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى الْإِسْلَامِ فَإِنْ أَبَوْا دَعَوْنَاهُمْ إِلَى الْجِزْيَةِ قَالَ وَ إِنْ كَانُوا مَجُوساً لَيْسُوا بِأَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ قَالَ سَوَاءٌ قَالَ وَ إِنْ كَانُوا مُشْرِكِي الْعَرَبِ وَ عَبَدَةَ الْأَوْثَانِ قَالَ سَوَاءٌ قَالَ أَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ الْقُرْآنِ تَقْرَؤُهُ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ اقْرَأْ قَاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَ لَا بِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَ لَا يُحَرِّمُونَ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ وَ رَسُولُهُ وَ لَا يَدِينُونَ دِينَ الْحَقِّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ حَتَّى يُعْطُوا الْجِزْيَةَ عَنْ يَدٍ وَ هُمْ صَاغِرُونَ فَاسْتِثْنَاءُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ اشْتِرَاطُهُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ فَهُمْ وَ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يُؤْتَوُا الْكِتَابَ سَوَاءٌ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ عَمَّنْ أَخَذْتَ ذَا قَالَ سَمِعْتُ النَّاسَ يَقُولُونَ قَالَ فَدَعْ ذَا فَإِنْ هُمْ أَبَوُا الْجِزْيَةَ فَقَاتَلْتَهُمْ فَظَهَرْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ كَيْفَ تَصْنَعُ بِالْغَنِيمَةِ قَالَ أُخْرِجُ الْخُمُسَ وَ أَقْسِمُ أَرْبَعَةَ أَخْمَاسٍ بَيْنَ مَنْ قَاتَلَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ أَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ الْخُمُسِ مَنْ تُعْطِيهِ قَالَ حَيْثُمَا سَمَّى اللَّهُ قَالَ فَقَرَأَ وَ اعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا غَنِمْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَأَنَّ لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُ وَ لِلرَّسُولِ وَ لِذِي الْقُرْبَى وَ الْيَتَامَى وَ الْمَسَاكِينِ وَ ابْنِ السَّبِيلِ قَالَ الَّذِي لِلرَّسُولِ مَنْ تُعْطِيهِ وَ مَنْ ذُو الْقُرْبَى قَالَ قَدِ اخْتَلَفَ فِيهِ الْفُقَهَاءُ فَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ قَرَابَةُ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ أَهْلُ بَيْتِهِ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمُ الْخَلِيفَةُ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ قَرَابَةُ الَّذِينَ قَاتَلُوا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ قَالَ فَأَيَّ ذَلِكَ تَقُولُ أَنْتَ قَالَ لَا أَدْرِي قَالَ فَأَرَاكَ لَا تَدْرِي فَدَعْ ذَا ثُمَّ قَالَ أَ رَأَيْتَ الْأَرْبَعَةَ أَخْمَاسٍ تَقْسِمُهَا بَيْنَ جَمِيعِ مَنْ قَاتَلَ عَلَيْهَا قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَقَدْ خَالَفْتَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي سِيرَتِهِ بَيْنِي وَ بَيْنَكَ فُقَهَاءُ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَ مَشِيخَتُهُمْ فَاسْأَلْهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَا يَخْتَلِفُونَ وَ لَا يَتَنَازَعُونَ فِي أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِنَّمَا صَالَحَ الْأَعْرَابَ عَلَى أَنْ يَدَعَهُمْ فِي دِيَارِهِمْ وَ لَا يُهَاجِرُوا عَلَى إِنْ دَهِمَهُ مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ دَهْمٌ أَنْ يَسْتَنْفِرَهُمْ فَيُقَاتِلَ بِهِمْ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ فِي الْغَنِيمَةِ نَصِيبٌ وَ أَنْتَ تَقُولُ بَيْنَ جَمِيعِهِمْ فَقَدْ خَالَفْتَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي كُلِّ مَا قُلْتَ فِي سِيرَتِهِ فِي الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَ مَعَ هَذَا مَا تَقُولُ فِي الصَّدَقَةِ فَقَرَأَ عَلَيْهِ الْآيَةَ إِنَّمَا الصَّدَقَاتُ لِلْفُقَرَاءِ وَ الْمَسَاكِينِ وَ الْعَامِلِينَ عَلَيْهَا إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ قَالَ نَعَمْ فَكَيْفَ تَقْسِمُهَا قَالَ أَقْسِمُهَا عَلَى ثَمَانِيَةِ أَجْزَاءٍ فَأُعْطِي كُلَّ جُزْءٍ مِنَ الثَّمَانِيَةِ جُزْءاً قَالَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ صِنْفٌ مِنْهُمْ عَشَرَةَ آلَافٍ وَ صِنْفٌ مِنْهُمْ رَجُلًا وَاحِداً أَوْ رَجُلَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثَةً جَعَلْتَ لِهَذَا الْوَاحِدِ مِثْلَ مَا جَعَلْتَ لِلْعَشَرَةِ آلَافٍ قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ وَ تَجْمَعُ صَدَقَاتِ أَهْلِ الْحَضَرِ وَ أَهْلِ الْبَوَادِي فَتَجْعَلُهُمْ فِيهَا سَوَاءً قَالَ نَعَمْ قَالَ فَقَدْ خَالَفْتَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي كُلِّ مَا قُلْتَ فِي سِيرَتِهِ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يَقْسِمُ صَدَقَةَ أَهْلِ الْبَوَادِي فِي أَهْلِ الْبَوَادِي وَ صَدَقَةَ أَهْلِ الْحَضَرِ فِي أَهْلِ الْحَضَرِ وَ لَا يَقْسِمُهُ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالسَّوِيَّةِ وَ إِنَّمَا يَقْسِمُهُ عَلَى قَدْرِ مَا يَحْضُرُهُ مِنْهُمْ وَ مَا يَرَى وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ فِي ذَلِكَ شَيْءٌ مُوَقَّتٌ مُوَظَّفٌ وَ إِنَّمَا يَصْنَعُ ذَلِكَ بِمَا يَرَى عَلَى قَدْرِ مَنْ يَحْضُرُهُ مِنْهُمْ فَإِنْ كَانَ فِي نَفْسِكَ مِمَّا قُلْتُ شَيْءٌ فَالْقَ فُقَهَاءَ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَا يَخْتَلِفُونَ فِي أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ كَذَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ ثُمَّ أَقْبَلَ عَلَى عَمْرِو بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ فَقَالَ لَهُ اتَّقِ اللَّهَ وَ أَنْتُمْ أَيُّهَا الرَّهْطُ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ فَإِنَّ أَبِي حَدَّثَنِي وَ كَانَ خَيْرَ أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ وَ أَعْلَمَهُمْ بِكِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ سُنَّةِ نَبِيِّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ مَنْ ضَرَبَ النَّاسَ بِسَيْفِهِ وَ دَعَاهُمْ إِلَى نَفْسِهِ وَ فِي الْمُسْلِمِينَ مَنْ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ مِنْهُ فَهُوَ ضَالٌّ مُتَكَلِّفٌ
diacritics_changes: Added full tashkeel throughout; source text had partial diacritics and abbreviated honorifics

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ أُذَيْنَةَ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ عُتْبَةَ الْهَاشِمِيِّ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: كُنْتُ قَاعِداً عِنْدَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ بِمَكَّةَ

narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Ibrahim al-Qummi (d. ~307 AH), author of Tafsir al-Qummi

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, father of Ali ibn Ibrahim

narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr al-Azdi (d. ~217 AH)
Umar ibn Udhayna al-Basri, a reliable Shia narrator
Zurara ibn Ayan (d. ~150 AH), companion of Imam al-Baqir and Imam al-Sadiq (AS)
Abd al-Karim ibn Utba al-Hashimi, companion of Imam al-Sadiq (AS) who witnessed this event
Imam Jafar al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)

en.text: Ali ibn Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Umar ibn Udhayna, from Zurara, from Abd al-Karim Utba al-Hashimi who said: I was sitting in the presence of Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) in Mecca when some people from the Mu'tazilites entered upon him, among them Amr ibn Ubayd, Wasil ibn Ata', Hafs ibn Salim the client of Ibn Hubayra, and some of their leaders. That was at the time of the murder of al-Walid and the discord among the people of Syria. They spoke and spoke at length and delivered prolonged speeches. Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said to them: You have overburdened me. Delegate your matter to one man from among you; let him speak with your arguments and be brief. So they delegated their matter to Amr ibn Ubayd, who spoke at length. Among what he said was: The people of Syria have killed their Caliph. God, Mighty and Majestic, has made them clash against each other, and God has scattered their affairs. We looked and found a man possessing religion, reason, nobility, standing, and the qualifications for the caliphate - he is Muhammad ibn Abdillah ibn al-Hasan. We want to unite behind him and pledge allegiance to him, then manifest ourselves with him. Whoever pledges allegiance with us is one of us, and we are with him; whoever withdraws from us we shall leave alone; and whoever sets himself against us we shall fight him and confront him for his aggression, returning him to the truth and its people. We wished to present this to you so that you might join us, for we cannot dispense with someone like you given your position and the abundance of your followers. When he finished, Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: Are all of you of the same view as Amr has stated? They said: Yes. So he praised God, extolled Him, and invoked blessings on the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family), then said: We are only displeased when God is disobeyed; when He is obeyed, we are pleased. Inform me, O Amr - if the community were to entrust you with its affairs and put you in charge without fighting or expense, and it were said to you: Put in charge whomever you wish - whom would you put in charge? He said: I would make it a consultative council among the Muslims. He said: Among all the Muslims? He said: Yes. He said: Among their jurists and best ones? He said: Yes. He said: Quraysh and others? He said: Yes. He said: Arabs and non-Arabs? He said: Yes. He said: Inform me, O Amr - do you befriend Abu Bakr and Umar, or do you disavow them? He said: I befriend them. He said: Then you have contradicted them. And what do you all say - do you befriend them or disavow them? They said: We befriend them. He said: O Amr! If you were a man who disavowed them, then it would be permissible for you to oppose them. But if you befriend them, then you have already contradicted them. For Umar pledged allegiance to Abu Bakr and swore loyalty to him without consulting anyone; then Abu Bakr returned it to him without consulting anyone; then Umar made it a consultative council among six, excluding all the Emigrants and Helpers except those six from Quraysh, and he bequeathed something among them which I do not think you or your companions would accept if you were making it a consultation among all the Muslims. He said: So what did he do? He said: He ordered Suhayb to lead the people in prayer for three days, and those six would deliberate - with no one among them except Ibn Umar for consultation, and he had no say in the matter. He bequeathed to those present from the Emigrants and Helpers: if three days passed before they completed it or pledged allegiance to a man, that they should strike the necks of all those six. And if four agreed before the three days passed and two opposed, that they should strike the necks of the two. Are you pleased with this - what you are making of the consultation among a group of Muslims? They said: No. Then he said: O Amr, leave that. What would you think if I pledged allegiance to your companion to whom you are calling me, and then the community united behind you, with no two men dissenting - and then you turned to the polytheists who neither submit to Islam nor pay the jizyah: would you and your companion have the knowledge to proceed according to the way of the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) with the polytheists in his wars? He said: Yes. He said: So what would you do? He said: We would call them to Islam; if they refuse, we would call them to the jizyah. He said: And if they were Magians who are not People of the Book? He said: Same. He said: And if they were Arab polytheists who worship idols? He said: Same. He said: Inform me about the Quran - do you recite it? He said: Yes. He said: Read: [9:29] Fight those who do not believe in God nor in the Last Day, who do not forbid what God and His Messenger have forbidden, and who do not practice the religion of truth among the People of the Book, until they pay the jizyah submissively while they are subdued. So God's exclusion and stipulation is from the People of the Book - so they and those who have not been given the Book are the same? He said: Yes. He said: From whom did you take that? He said: I heard the people saying it. He said: Leave that. If they refuse the jizyah and you fight them and overcome them, how would you handle the war spoils? He said: I would extract the one-fifth (khums) and distribute the four-fifths among those who fought for it. He said: Inform me about the khums - whom would you give it to? He said: To whomever God has named. Then he recited: [8:41] Know that whatever war spoils you gain, a fifth of it belongs to God and to the Messenger and to the near of kin, the orphans, the destitute, and the wayfarer. He said: That which is for the Messenger - whom would you give it to, and who are the near of kin? He said: The jurists have differed about it. Some say the near kin of the Prophet and the People of his Household; others say the Caliph; others say the near kin of those Muslims who fought. He said: So which of these do you say it is? He said: I do not know. He said: I see you do not know - so leave that. Then he said: What would you think of the four-fifths - would you distribute them among all those who fought? He said: Yes. He said: Then you have contradicted the Messenger of God in his practice. Between me and you are the jurists and elders of Medina - ask them, for they do not differ or dispute that the Messenger of God made a treaty with the Bedouins on condition that he would leave them in their homes and they would not emigrate - on the condition that if he were raided by an enemy he would call upon them and fight with them, but they would have no share in the war spoils. And you say it should be among all of them - so you have contradicted the Messenger of God in everything you said about his practice with the polytheists. And along with this - what do you say about the charity? So he recited to him the verse: [9:60] The charities are only for the poor and the destitute and those who work to administer them... to the end of the verse. He said: Yes. How would you distribute it? He said: I would divide it into eight portions, giving each of the eight categories one portion. He said: And if one category were ten thousand while another category was one man, or two, or three, you would give this one man the same as the ten thousand? He said: Yes. He said: And you would combine the charities of urban people and rural people and treat them equally in it? He said: Yes. He said: Then you have contradicted the Messenger of God in everything you said about his practice. The Messenger of God used to distribute the charities of rural people among rural people, and the charities of urban people among urban people, and did not distribute them equally - but distributed it in proportion to how many from them were present and what he judged fit, and there was nothing fixed or prescribed upon him regarding that. Rather, he did that according to his judgment, in proportion to how many from them were present. So if there is anything in your heart from what I have said, go and meet the jurists of Medina, for they do not differ that the Messenger of God used to do exactly that. Then he turned to Amr ibn Ubayd and said to him: Fear God, and you too, O group - fear God. For my father narrated to me - and he was the best of the people of the earth and the most knowledgeable of them in the Book of God, Mighty and Majestic, and the Sunnah of His Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) - that the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) said: Whoever strikes the people with his sword and calls them to himself, while among the Muslims there is one more knowledgeable than him - he is astray, a pretender.
en.summary: This hadith records a pivotal encounter in Mecca between Imam al-Sadiq (AS) and leading Mu'tazilite theologians, including Amr ibn Ubayd and Wasil ibn Ata', who invited the Imam to support Muhammad ibn Abdillah ibn al-Hasan in a political uprising following the murder of the Umayyad Caliph al-Walid II in 126 AH. The Imam, through a masterful Socratic dialogue, exposed the Mu'tazilites' ignorance of Islamic jurisprudence on warfare, spoils of war, khums, and charity - demonstrating that their proposed leader could not implement the Prophetic way. He concluded with the prophetic teaching that anyone who calls people to himself by force while a more knowledgeable person exists is astray, a direct refutation of any claim to leadership that bypasses divinely-appointed knowledge.

key_terms.al-Mutazila: The Mu'tazilites - a rationalist theological school of early Islam emphasizing free will and divine justice
key_terms.al-shura: Shura - consultation; the Mu'tazilite principle for selecting political leadership
key_terms.al-jizyah: Jizyah - the poll tax levied on non-Muslim subjects under Islamic governance
key_terms.al-khums: Khums - the one-fifth religious tax on war spoils, whose distribution is debated
key_terms.dall mutakallif: Astray and a pretender - the Imam's description of one who claims leadership without requisite knowledge

en.seo_question: What did Imam al-Sadiq say to the Mu'tazilites about political leadership and jurisprudence?

ur.text: علی بن ابراہیم نے اپنے والد سے، انہوں نے ابن ابی عمیر سے، انہوں نے عمر بن اذینہ سے، انہوں نے زرارہ سے، انہوں نے عبد الکریم عتبہ الہاشمی سے روایت کی کہ میں مکہ میں ابو عبداللہ علیہ السلام کے پاس بیٹھا ہوا تھا۔ معتزلہ کے چند لوگ آئے جن میں عمرو بن عبید، واصل بن عطاء اور ان کے چند سردار شامل تھے۔ یہ ولید کے قتل کے بعد کا وقت تھا۔ امام نے ان کا مطالبہ سن کر کہا کہ ایک نمائندہ مقرر کریں۔ عمرو بن عبید نے طویل تقریر کی اور محمد بن عبداللہ بن الحسن کی حمایت کی درخواست کی۔ امام نے حمد و ثنا کے بعد سوالات سے ثابت کیا کہ معتزلہ کا طریقہ ابوبکر و عمر سے متصادم ہے اور غنائم، خمس اور زکوۃ کی نبوی تقسیم سے وہ ناواقف ہیں۔ آخر میں فرمایا کہ رسول اللہ نے فرمایا: جو تلوار سے لوگوں کو اپنی طرف بلائے جبکہ مسلمانوں میں اس سے زیادہ عالم ہو، وہ گمراہ ہے۔
ur.summary: یہ حدیث امام صادق علیہ السلام اور معتزلہ کے سرداروں کے درمیان مکہ میں ایک تاریخی مناظرے کا بیان ہے۔ امام نے سقراطی انداز سے ان کی جہالت ثابت کی اور قیادت کے لیے ربانی علم کی ضرورت واضح کی۔

key_terms.الجزية: جزیہ - غیر مسلم رعایا کا ٹیکس
key_terms.الخمس: خمس - پانچویں حصے کا دینی ٹیکس

ur.seo_question: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے معتزلہ کو کیا جواب دیا؟

tr.text: Ali b. Ibrahim, babasindan, Ibn Ebi Umeyr'den, Omer b. Uzayne'den, Zurare'den, Abdulkerim Utbe el-Hasimi'den rivayet etti. Mekke'de Ebu Abdullah (a.s.) ile birlikte oturuyordum. Mutezileden bir grup geldi: Amr b. Ubeyd, Vasil b. Ata, Hafs b. Salim ve bazi liderler. Velid'in oldurulmesinden hemen sonraydi. Uzun konustular. Ebu Abdullah bir temsilci secmelerini istedi. Amr b. Ubeyd secildi ve Muhammed b. Abdullah b. el-Hasan'i halife olarak destekledi, Imam'i katilmaya davet etti. Imam Allah'a hamd etti ve dedi ki: Yalnizca Allah'a isyan edildiginde kizariz. Bir dizi soruyla Amr'in bilgisizligini ortaya koydu: shura yontemi Ebu Bekir ve Omer ile catisiyor; ganimetler, humus ve zekat konusunda sunnet bilinmiyor. Sonunda su hadisi aktardi: Muslumanlarin arasinda daha bilgili biri varken kiligiyla insanlari kendine cagiran sapkindir.
tr.summary: Bu hadis, Imam Sadik ile Mutezile liderlerinin Mekke'deki tarihi karsilasmalarini anlatir. Imam, soru-cevapla onlarin hukuk bilgisizligini ortaya koydu ve mesruiyetin ilmi temele dayandigi gosterdi.

key_terms.el-Mutezile: Mutezile - erken Islam'in akil merkezli kelam okulu
key_terms.el-sura: Sura - istisare, liderlik secimi icin temel ilke
key_terms.el-cizye: Cizye - Islam yonetimi altindaki gayrimuslimlerden alinan vergi

tr.seo_question: Imam Sadik Mutezile'ye nasil cevap verdi?

fa.text: علی بن ابراهیم از پدرش از ابن ابی‌عمیر از عمر بن اذینه از زراره از عبدالکریم عتبه هاشمی روایت کرد که گفت: در مکه نزد ابو عبدالله علیه‌السلام نشسته بودم که گروهی از معتزله وارد شدند، از جمله عمرو بن عبید، واصل بن عطا، حفص بن سالم و برخی سرانشان. این پس از کشته شدن ولید بود. آنها دیر صحبت کردند. امام فرمود: یک نماینده برگزینید. عمرو بن عبید معرفی شد و گفت محمد بن عبدالله بن الحسن شایسته خلافت است. امام خدا را ستود و فرمود: ما تنها آنگاه ناراضی‌ایم که نافرمانی خدا شود. با پرسش‌های متوالی جهل آنان را آشکار کرد: روش شورا با سیره ابوبکر و عمر در تضاد است؛ در مورد غنیمت، خمس و زکات از سنت نبوی بی‌خبرند. در پایان فرمود: رسول خدا فرمود هر کس با شمشیر مردم را به خود بخواند درحالی‌که داناتری در میانشان هست، گمراه و متکلف است.
fa.summary: این حدیث دیدار تاریخی امام صادق با سران معتزله در مکه را روایت می‌کند. امام با گفت‌وگویی سقراطی جهل آنان را آشکار و ثابت کرد رهبری مشروع نیازمند علم الهی است.

key_terms.المعتزلة: معتزله - مکتب عقل‌گرای کلامی اسلام اولیه

fa.seo_question: امام صادق در برابر دعوت معتزله چه فرمود؟

id.text: Ali bin Ibrahim, dari ayahnya, dari Ibn Abi Umayr, dari Umar bin Udzayna, dari Zurara, dari Abd al-Karim Utba al-Hashimi yang berkata: Aku sedang duduk di hadapan Abu Abdillah (a.s.) di Mekah ketika datanglah beberapa orang dari kaum Mu'tazilah, di antaranya Amr bin Ubayd, Washil bin Atha', Hafsh bin Salim dan beberapa pemimpin mereka. Ini terjadi setelah terbunuhnya al-Walid. Mereka berbicara panjang lebar. Abu Abdillah berkata: tunjuklah satu orang. Mereka menunjuk Amr bin Ubayd yang berbicara panjang dan mengatakan Muhammad bin Abdullah bin al-Hasan layak sebagai khalifah, mengundang Imam untuk bergabung. Imam memuji Allah dan berkata: kami hanya murka ketika Allah didurhakai. Dengan serangkaian pertanyaan Imam mengungkap kebodohan Amr: metode syura bertentangan dengan pratik Abu Bakar dan Umar; tidak memahami sunnah Nabi tentang ghanimah, khums, dan zakat. Akhirnya beliau menyampaikan hadis Nabi: siapa yang memukul manusia dengan pedangnya dan menyeru mereka kepada dirinya, sementara di antara kaum muslimin ada yang lebih berilmu, maka dia sesat.
id.summary: Hadis ini mengisahkan pertemuan bersejarah di Mekah antara Imam al-Shadiq dan para pemimpin Mu'tazilah yang mengundang Imam mendukung pemberontakan. Melalui dialog Sokrates, Imam mengungkap kebodohan mereka dalam fiqih dan membuktikan pemimpin yang sah harus memiliki ilmu ilahi.

key_terms.al-Mu'tazilah: Mu'tazilah - aliran teologi rasionalis Islam awal
key_terms.al-jizyah: Jizyah - pajak atas non-muslim di bawah pemerintahan Islam

id.seo_question: Apa tanggapan Imam al-Shadiq terhadap ajakan Mu'tazilah?

bn.text: আলী ইবনে ইব্রাহীম তাঁর পিতা থেকে, তিনি ইবনে আবী উমায়র থেকে, তিনি উমার ইবনে উযায়না থেকে, তিনি যুরারা থেকে, তিনি আব্দুল কারীম উতবা আল-হাশিমী থেকে বর্ণনা করেছেন - তিনি বলেন: আমি মক্কায় আবু আবদিল্লাহ আলাইহিস সালামের কাছে বসেছিলাম। মুতাযিলাদের কিছু লোক প্রবেশ করল, তাদের মধ্যে আমর ইবনে উবায়দ, ওয়াসিল ইবনে আতা, হাফস ইবনে সালেম এবং কিছু নেতা। আল-ওয়ালীদের হত্যার পরের সময়। তারা দীর্ঘ বক্তব্য রাখল। ইমাম একজনকে মনোনীত করতে বললেন। আমর ইবনে উবায়দ মনোনীত হলো এবং মুহাম্মাদ ইবনে আব্দিল্লাহ ইবনে আল-হাসানকে খলীফার জন্য উপযুক্ত বলে ইমামকে আমন্ত্রণ জানাল। ইমাম আল্লাহর প্রশংসা করে বললেন: আমরা তখনই অসন্তুষ্ট হই যখন আল্লাহর নাফরমানি হয়। প্রশ্নের মাধ্যমে আমর-এর অজ্ঞতা প্রমাণ করলেন: শূরার পদ্ধতি আবু বকর ও উমারের সাথে সাংঘর্ষিক; গনীমত, খুমস ও যাকাত সম্পর্কে নবীর সুন্নত সম্পর্কে অজ্ঞ। পরিশেষে বললেন: রাসূল বলেছেন যে ব্যক্তি তলোয়ার দিয়ে মানুষকে নিজের দিকে ডাকে অথচ মুসলমানদের মাঝে তার চেয়ে বেশি জ্ঞানী কেউ আছে - সে পথভ্রষ্ট।
bn.summary: এই হাদীসে মক্কায় ইমাম আস-সাদিক (আ.) ও মুতাযিলার নেতাদের ঐতিহাসিক সাক্ষাতের বর্ণনা। ইমাম প্রমাণ করলেন বৈধ নেতৃত্বের জন্য ঐশ্বরিক জ্ঞান প্রয়োজন।

key_terms.al-Mu'tazila: মুতাযিলা - প্রাথমিক ইসলামের যুক্তিবাদী ধর্মতাত্ত্বিক স্কুল

bn.seo_question: ইমাম আস-সাদিক মুতাযিলাকে কী জবাব দিয়েছিলেন?

es.text: Ali ibn Ibrahim, de su padre, de Ibn Abi Umayr, de Umar ibn Udhaina, de Zurara, de Abd al-Karim Utba al-Hashimi quien dijo: Estaba sentado en presencia de Abu Abdillah en La Meca cuando entraron personas de los Mu'tazilitas, entre ellos Amr ibn Ubayd, Wasil ibn Ata', Hafs ibn Salim y algunos de sus lideres. Era el momento posterior al asesinato de al-Walid. Hablaron mucho. Abu Abdillah pidio que delegaran en uno. Delegaron en Amr ibn Ubayd quien dijo que Muhammad ibn Abdillah ibn al-Hasan era apto para el califato e invito al Imam a unirse. El Imam alabo a Dios y dijo: Solo nos disgustamos cuando Dios es desobedecido. A traves de preguntas expuso la ignorancia de Amr: su metodo de shura contradice la practica de Abu Bakr y Umar; desconoce la Sunna sobre el botin de guerra, khums y zakat. Finalmente transmitio el hadiz: Quien golpee a la gente con su espada y los llame a si mismo, mientras entre los musulmanes hay quien es mas sabio, es un extraviado.
es.summary: Este hadiz narra el historico encuentro en La Meca entre el Imam al-Sadiq y los lideres mu'tazilitas. A traves de un dialogo socratico, el Imam expuso su ignorancia juridica y demostro que el liderazgo legitimo requiere conocimiento divino.

key_terms.los Mu'tazilitas: Mu'tazilitas - escuela teologica racionalista del Islam temprano
key_terms.la shura: Shura - consulta, principio para la seleccion de liderazgo
key_terms.la jizyah: Jizyah - impuesto sobre los no musulmanes bajo gobierno islamico
key_terms.el khums: Khums - impuesto religioso de la quinta parte
key_terms.extraviado e impostor: Dall mutakallif - descripcion del Imam para quien reclama liderazgo sin conocimiento

es.seo_question: Que dijo el Imam al-Sadiq a los mu'tazilitas sobre el liderazgo politico?

fr.text: Ali ibn Ibrahim, de son pere, d'Ibn Abi Umayr, d'Umar ibn Udhaina, de Zurara, d'Abd al-Karim Utba al-Hashimi qui dit: J'etais assis aupres d'Abu Abdillah a La Mecque lorsque des gens des Mu'tazilites entrerent, parmi eux Amr ibn Ubayd, Wasil ibn Ata', Hafs ibn Salim et quelques-uns de leurs chefs. C'etait apres l'assassinat d'al-Walid. Ils parlerent longuement. Abu Abdillah demanda de deleguer un representant. Ils designerent Amr ibn Ubayd qui dit que Muhammad ibn Abdillah ibn al-Hasan etait apte au califat et invita l'Imam a se joindre a eux. L'Imam loua Dieu et dit: Nous ne sommes insatisfaits que lorsque Dieu est desobeyi. A travers des questions, il exposa l'ignorance d'Amr: sa methode de shura contredit la pratique d'Abu Bakr et Umar; il ignore la Sunna sur le butin de guerre, le khums et la zakat. Il conclut: Quiconque frappe les gens de son epee et les appelle a lui, alors qu'il existe parmi les musulmans quelqu'un de plus savant, est egare.
fr.summary: Ce hadith relate la rencontre historique a La Mecque entre l'Imam al-Sadiq et les chefs mu'tazilites. Par un dialogue socratique, l'Imam revela leur ignorance juridique et demontra que le leadership legitime requiert une science divine.

key_terms.les Mu'tazilites: Mu'tazilites - ecole theologique rationaliste de l'Islam primitif
key_terms.la shura: Shura - consultation, principe pour la selection du leadership
key_terms.la jizyah: Jizyah - impot sur les non-musulmans sous gouvernement islamique

fr.seo_question: Que dit l'Imam al-Sadiq aux mu'tazilites sur le leadership?

de.text: Ali ibn Ibrahim, von seinem Vater, von Ibn Abi Umayr, von Umar ibn Udhaina, von Zurara, von Abd al-Karim Utba al-Hashimi, der berichtete: Ich sass bei Abu Abdillah in Mekka, als einige Leute von den Mu'taziliten hereinkamen, darunter Amr ibn Ubayd, Wasil ibn Ata', Hafs ibn Salim und einige ihrer Anfuehrer. Dies war nach der Ermordung al-Walids. Sie sprachen viel. Abu Abdillah bat sie, einen Vertreter zu benennen. Sie benannten Amr ibn Ubayd, der sagte, Muhammad ibn Abdillah ibn al-Hasan sei geeignet als Kalif, und lud den Imam ein, sich anzuschliessen. Der Imam lobte Gott und sagte: Wir sind nur unzufrieden, wenn Gott nicht gehorcht wird. Durch Fragen legte er die Unwissenheit von Amr bloss: seine Shura-Methode widerspricht der Praxis von Abu Bakr und Umar; er kennt die Sunna nicht hinsichtlich Kriegsbeute, Khums und Zakat. Abschliessend sagte er: Wer die Menschen mit seinem Schwert schlaegt und sie zu sich selbst ruft, waehrend unter den Muslimen jemand Gelehrteres ist, der ist irregeganegen.
de.summary: Dieser Hadith schildert die historische Begegnung in Mekka zwischen Imam al-Sadiq und mu'tazilitischen Fuehrern. In einem sokratischen Dialog legte der Imam ihre Unwissenheit bloss und bewies, dass legitime Fuehrung gottliches Wissen erfordert.

key_terms.die Mu'taziliten: Mu'taziliten - rationalistische theologische Schule des fruehen Islams
key_terms.Dschizya: Jizyah - Steuer fuer Nicht-Muslime unter islamischer Herrschaft
key_terms.Khums: Khums - religioese Steuer des fuenften Teils

de.seo_question: Was antwortete Imam al-Sadiq den Mu'taziliten ueber politische Fuehrung?

ru.text: Ali ibn Ibrahim, ot svoego otca, ot Ibn Abi Umayr, ot Umara ibn Udajny, ot Zurara, ot Abd al-Karima Utby al-Hashimi, kotoryj rasskazal: Ya sidel u Abu Abdillaha v Mekke, kogda voshli nekotorye lyudi iz mutazilitov - Amr ibn Ubajd, Vasil ibn Ata, Hafs ibn Salim i nekotorye predvoditeli. Eto bylo posle ubijstva al-Valida. Oni dolgo govorili. Abu Abdillah poprosil naznachit odnog predstavitelya. Naznachili Amra ibn Ubajda, kotoryj skazal, chto Muhammad ibn Abdillah ibn al-Hasan prigoden dlya halifata i priglasil Imama primknut. Imam proslavil Allaha i skazal: My nedovolny lish togda, kogda Allahu ne povinuyutsya. Cherez voprosy obnazhil nevezhestvo Amra: metod shury protivorechit praktike Abu Bakra i Umara; on ne znaet sunny o vojnnoj dobych, humse i zakate. V zaklyuchenie peredal hadis: Kto byot lyudej mechem i zovyot k sebe, togda kak est bolee znayushchij, tot zabludshij.
ru.summary: Etot hadis povestvuet ob istoricheskom stolknovenii v Mekke mezhdu Imamom as-Sadikom i liderami mutazilitov. Cherez sokraticheskij dialog Imam obnazhil ih nevezhestvo v yurisprudencii i dokazal, chto zakonnoye rukovodstvo trebuet bozhestvennogo znanya.

key_terms.hums: Hums - religioznyj nalog v odnu pyatuyu

ru.seo_question: Chto otvetil Imam as-Sadik mutazilitam o politicheskom rukovodstve?

zh.text: 阿里·本·易卜拉欣从其父传述，从伊本·阿比·欧迈尔，从欧麦尔·本·奥扎伊纳，从祖拉拉，从阿卜杜勒-卡里姆·欧特拜·哈希米传述——他说：我坐在麦加阿布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）身旁时，一些穆尔太齐赖派的人进来了，其中有阿姆鲁·本·欧百德、瓦绥勒·本·阿塔、哈夫绥·本·萨利姆和一些首领。这是瓦利德被杀之后。他们发表了长篇大论。阿布·阿卜杜拉让他们推选一人。他们推选阿姆鲁·本·欧百德，他提出穆罕默德·本·阿卜杜拉·本·哈桑适合担任哈里发，邀请伊玛目加入。伊玛目赞美真主后说：只有当真主被悖逆时我们才生气。通过一系列问题，他揭露了阿姆鲁的无知：他的协商方式与艾布·伯克尔和欧麦尔的做法相悖；不了解战利品、胡姆斯和天课的先知圣行。最后他传达了先知的教导：谁用剑打击众人并号召他们追随自己，而穆斯林中有比他更有学识的人，他就是迷途者。
zh.summary: 这段圣训记载了伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）在麦加与穆尔太齐赖领袖们的历史性会面。伊玛目通过苏格拉底式对话，揭露了他们在律法方面的无知，证明了合法领导需要神圣知识。

key_terms.穆尔太齐赖派: 早期伊斯兰理性主义神学学派

zh.seo_question: 伊玛目萨迪格如何回应穆尔太齐赖派的邀请？

key_phrases.phrase_en: May Allah bless him and his family

similar_content_hints.description: A hadith reporting the Imam's teaching that political authority belongs to the most knowledgeable, and that those who seize leadership without divine appointment are astray.
Hadiths in al-Kafi's Book of Proof discussing the necessity of an Imam with complete knowledge of Islamic law for governance to be legitimate.
Narrations about encounters between the Imams and Mu'tazilite or other rationalist theologians, where the Imam's superior knowledge is demonstrated.
